title: cephaelis schraderoides
intro: Cephaelis schraderoides, also known as Schrader's honeysuckle or Schrader's woodbine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Central and South America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cephaelis schraderoides include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. Cephaelis schraderoides can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, typically made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, and is also used in herbal remedies to treat a range of ailments.
